name: Kouloura
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the yeast in warm water.
2. Mix the remaining ingredients, except sesame seeds, using more or less flour as necessary to form a workable dough.
3. Knead the dough until smooth. Shape into a ball and place in an oiled bowl.
4. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk.
5. Punch down the dough and turn out onto a floured surface to shape.
6. Form the dough into a thick rope about 20 inches in length, slightly tapered at each end.
7. Place the rope in the shape of a ring on a greased baking sheet, overlapping and tucking under the ends.
8. Cover and let rise again.
9. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
10. Brush the bread with water and sprinkle with sesame seeds.
11. Bake until the bread is golden brown, about 30 minutes.
Variation - Elioti (Olive Bread):
1. Prepare one quantity of Kouloura dough as directed above.
2. During the first rising, saute a medium onion in 1 tablespoon olive oil until transparent.
3. Lightly smash 1 cup of Greek black olives with a wooden mallet or knife handle to split; remove pits from the olives.
4. Mix the olives and onions together and let cool.
5. After punching down the dough, turn onto a floured surface and roll out to form a rectangle about 1/2 inch thick.
6. Cover the rectangle with the onion and olive mixture, leaving a border around the edges.
7. Starting at one long end, roll up the dough and shape into a loaf.
8. Press the edges closed.
9. Place the loaf on a greased baking sheet and slash diagonally in three or four places.
10. Cover and let rise again until double in size.
11. Bake in a preheated 375°F (190°C) oven about 35-40 minutes.
